name: HALOZYME THERAPEUTICS, INC.
business: Halozyme Therapeutics is a biopharmaceutical company that develops and licenses drug delivery technologies, including ENHANZE (rHuPH20 enzyme), Hypercon, and Surf Bio technologies, to facilitate subcutaneous delivery of injected drugs. It also develops, manufactures, and commercializes drug-device combination products using its advanced auto-injector technologies, and markets proprietary products Hylenex and XYOSTED. The company licenses its technologies to biopharmaceutical partners and sells products through wholesale distributors to hospitals and retail pharmacies.

exposures.sentence: We license our ENHANZE technology to form collaborations with biopharmaceutical companies that develop and/or market drugs requiring or benefiting from injection via the SC route of administration.
We also develop, manufacture and commercialize, for ourselves or with our partners, drug-device combination products using our advanced auto-injector technologies.

catalysts.event: Roche nominated a new undisclosed non-exclusive target to be studied using ENHANZE
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, Roche nominated a new undisclosed non-exclusive target to be studied using ENHANZE.

catalysts.event: Roche received positive CHMP opinion recommending label update for Phesgo to allow home administration
catalysts.sentence: In April 2025, Roche received a positive opinion from the European Medicines Agency’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use recommending an update to the European Union (EU) label for Phesgo for human epidermal growth factor receptor 2-positive breast cancer.

catalysts.event: argenx received Japan approval for VYVDURA prefilled syringe for self-injection
catalysts.sentence: In September 2025, argenx received approval from the Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare in Japan for VYVDURA prefilled syringe for self-injection for the treatment of adult patients with generalized myasthenia gravis and adult patients with chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy.

catalysts.event: argenx received European Commission approval of VYVGART SC with ENHANZE for CIDP
catalysts.sentence: In June 2025, argenx announced European Commission approval of VYVGART SC with ENHANZE for the treatment of adult patients with progressive or relapsing active chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy after prior treatment with corticosteroids or immunoglobulins.

catalysts.event: argenx initiated Phase 1 study to evaluate ARGX-213 with ENHANZE
catalysts.sentence: In May 2025, argenx initiated a Phase 1 study to evaluate ARGX-213 with ENHANZE.

catalysts.event: argenx received FDA approval of VYVGART Hytrulo prefilled syringe for self-injection
catalysts.sentence: In April 2025, argenx received FDA approval of VYVGART Hytrulo prefilled syringe for self-injection for the treatment of adult patients with generalized myasthenia gravis who are anti-acetylcholine receptor antibody positive and adult patients with chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy.

catalysts.event: Janssen received FDA approval of DARZALEX FASPRO in combination for newly diagnosed multiple myeloma transplant-ineligible
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, Janssen announced the FDA approved DARZALEX FASPRO (daratumumab and hyaluronidase-fihj) in combination with bortezomib, lenalidomide and dexamethasone for the treatment of adult patients with newly diagnosed multiple myeloma who are ineligible for autologous stem cell transplant.

catalysts.event: Janssen received FDA approval of RYBREVANT FASPRO for EGFR-mutated NSCLC
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, Janssen announced the FDA approved RYBREVANT FASPRO (amivantamab and hyaluronidase-lpuj) for the treatment of patients with epidermal growth factor receptor-mutated locally advanced or metastatic non-small cell lung cancer.

catalysts.event: Janssen received NMPA approval in China for RYBREVANT FASPRO
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, Janssen received approval from the National Medical Products Administration in China for RYBREVANT FASPRO for the first-line treatment of adult patients with advanced non-small cell lung cancer.

catalysts.event: Janssen received Japan approval for RYBROFAZ (amivantamab) with ENHANZE
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, Janssen received approval from the Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare in Japan for RYBROFAZ (amivantamab) with ENHANZE for the first-line treatment of adult patients with advanced non-small cell lung cancer.

catalysts.event: Janssen received FDA approval of DARZALEX FASPRO as single treatment for high-risk smoldering multiple myeloma
catalysts.sentence: In November 2025, Janssen announced the FDA approved DARZALEX FASPRO (daratumumab and hyaluronidase-fihj) co-formulated with ENHANZE, as single treatment of adult patients with high-risk smoldering multiple myeloma.

catalysts.event: Janssen received European Commission approval of DARZALEX SC as monotherapy for smoldering multiple myeloma
catalysts.sentence: In July 2025, Janssen announced the European Commission approved a new indication for DARZALEX SC as a monotherapy for the treatment of adult patients with smoldering multiple myeloma at high risk of developing multiple myeloma.

catalysts.event: Janssen received European Commission marketing authorization for RYBREVANT SC with ENHANZE in combination with LAZCLUZE
catalysts.sentence: In April 2025, Janssen received European Commission marketing authorization of the SC formulation of RYBREVANT (amivantamab) with ENHANZE, in combination with LAZCLUZE (lazertinib), for the first-line treatment of adult patients with advanced non-small cell lung cancer.

catalysts.event: Janssen received European Commission approval for indication extension of DARZALEX SC in combination with bortezomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone
catalysts.sentence: In April 2025, Janssen received European Commission approval for an indication extension of DARZALEX SC in combination with bortezomib, lenalidomide, and dexamethasone for the treatment of adult patients with newly diagnosed multiple myeloma regardless of transplant eligibility.

catalysts.event: Takeda entered new collaboration with Halozyme for vedolizumab ENHANZE formulation
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, we and Takeda entered into a new global collaboration and exclusive license agreement which provides Takeda with access to ENHANZE for use with vedolizumab, marketed globally as ENTYVIO, for the treatment of adults with moderately to severely active Crohn’s disease or ulcerative colitis.

catalysts.event: Takeda received Japan approval for HYQVIA SC with ENHANZE for CIDP and MMN
catalysts.sentence: In June 2025, Takeda announced the Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare in Japan approved HYQVIA SC with ENHANZE for treatment of patients with chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy and multifocal motor neuropathy.

catalysts.event: Takeda received Health Canada expanded approval for HYQVIA for CIDP maintenance
catalysts.sentence: In March 2025, Takeda announced Health Canada expanded the marketing authorization for HYQVIA to include chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy as a maintenance therapy after stabilization with intravenous immunoglobulin to prevent relapse of neuromuscular disability and impairment in adults.

catalysts.event: BMS received European Commission approval of Opdivo SC
catalysts.sentence: In May 2025, BMS received European Commission approval of Opdivo SC, the SC formulation of Opdivo (nivolumab) developed with ENHANZE, for use across multiple adult solid tumors.

catalysts.event: Acumen announced positive Phase 1 results for sabirnetug with ENHANZE
catalysts.sentence: In March 2025, Acumen announced top-line results from a Phase 1 study of sabirnetug (ACU193) with ENHANZE comparing the pharmacokinetics between SC and IV administrations in healthy volunteers that demonstrated weekly SC administration of sabirnetug was well-tolerated with systematic exposure supporting further clinical development.

catalysts.event: Halozyme entered commercial license and supply agreement with Viatris for selatogrel auto-injector
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, we entered into a commercial license and supply agreement with Viatris under which we license and supply an auto-injector product for self-administered SC selatogrel for the treatment of acute myocardial infarction in adult patients.

catalysts.event: Halozyme entered non-exclusive collaboration with Merus for petosemtamab ENHANZE
catalysts.sentence: In November 2025, we and Merus entered into a non-exclusive global collaboration and license agreement that provides Merus access to ENHANZE for the development and potential commercialization of SC formulation of petosemtamab, an epidermal growth factor receptor and leucine-rich repeat-containing G-protein coupled receptor 5 bispecific antibody, for the treatment of head and neck cancer.

catalysts.event: Halozyme entered non-exclusive collaboration with Skye Bioscience for nimacimab ENHANZE
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, we and Skye Bioscience entered into a non-exclusive global collaboration and license agreement that provides Skye Bioscience access to ENHANZE for the development and potential commercialization of SC formulation of nimacimab for the treatment of obesity.

catalysts.event: Halozyme completed acquisition of Elektrofi (renamed Halozyme Hypercon)
catalysts.sentence: In November 2025, we completed the acquisition of Elektrofi, Inc., subsequently renamed Halozyme Hypercon, Inc., resulting in an expansion of our drug delivery technology portfolio.

catalysts.event: Halozyme completed acquisition of Surf Bio (renamed Halozyme Surf Bio)
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, we completed the acquisition of Surf Bio, Inc., subsequently renamed Halozyme Surf Bio, Inc., resulting in an expansion of our drug delivery technology portfolio.

catalysts.event: Halozyme sold $750M 0.875% Convertible Senior Notes due 2032 and $750M 0.00% Convertible Senior Notes due 2031
catalysts.sentence: In November 2025, we completed the sale of $750.0 million aggregate principal amount of the 2031 Convertible Notes and $750.0 million aggregate principal amount of the 2032 Convertible Notes.

catalysts.event: Halozyme initiated third $250 million share repurchase tranche
catalysts.sentence: In June 2025, we initiated the third $250 million share repurchase tranche under the $750 million approved program from February 2024.

catalysts.event: Halozyme announced second $250 million share repurchase
catalysts.sentence: In May 2025, we announced a second $250 million share repurchase under the $750 million approved program from February 2024.

catalysts.event: Halozyme filed patent infringement lawsuit against Merck for Keytruda SC
catalysts.sentence: In April 2025, we filed a patent infringement lawsuit against Merck in the U.S. District Court in New Jersey.
